doc:
Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_nexttozero (mpfr/src/next.c L45-L85). Compute the immediate-neighbor representable value of x in the direction of zero, at x's own precision. No rounding occurs (the 'next' is unique in the precision-defined lattice), so the TS port returns a bare MPFR rather than a Result {value, ternary}.

C behaviour (mpfr/src/next.c L45-L85):
  - Singular branch:
      * Inf -> mpfr_setmax(x, __gmpfr_emax): largest finite at emax, sign preserved.
          mantissa becomes 2^prec - 1 (all prec bits set), exp = emax.
      * Zero -> MPFR_CHANGE_SIGN then mpfr_setmin(x, __gmpfr_emin): the sign
          FLIPS, then the smallest representable at emin. This matches
          IEEE-754 nextDown(+0) = -minSubnormal and nextUp(-0) = +minSubnormal
          (mpfr_nexttozero is called from mpfr_nextbelow on positive x, where
          x>0 stepping toward zero hits +0 then sign-flips to -smallest).
      * NaN -> the C asserts (the function is undefined on NaN at the C
          level because callers nextabove/nextbelow/nexttoward strip NaN
          first). The TS port is public-facing and defensively propagates
          NaN -> NaN.
  - Normal branch (mpfr/src/next.c L62-L84):
      * Subtract one ULP from the mantissa (at the precision boundary):
          mant -= 1.
      * If mant becomes 2^(prec-1) - 1 (i.e. the MSB at position prec-1 is
          no longer set; this only happens when mant was exactly
          2^(prec-1), an exact power of two): the value needs
          renormalization.
          - If exp == emin (= -(2^30 - 1)): set to signed zero (same sign).
          - Else: exp -= 1, mant = 2^prec - 1 (all prec bits set).
      * Otherwise (MSB still set): mant -= 1 is the result.

Ternary semantics: not applicable -- mpfr_nexttozero is exact in the
precision-defined lattice (the 'next' value IS the next representable
at x's prec). The TS port returns MPFR, not Result.

emin/emax: we use EMIN_DEFAULT = -(2^30 - 1) and EMAX_DEFAULT = 2^30 - 1
(mpfr/src/mpfr.h L231-L232), the same defaults set_exp.ts uses. This
matches what libmpfr does in the golden driver (which never calls
mpfr_set_emin/emax).
divergence_from_c:
1. Return shape: C mutates x in place; TS returns a fresh MPFR.
2. NaN handling: C asserts on NaN inputs (the function is private to its callers, which all NaN-check upstream); TS defensively propagates NaN -> NaN since it is exposed as part of the public surface.
3. Global flag side effects (the C function does not set __gmpfr_flags, so this divergence is empty -- noted for completeness).
4. emin/emax: C uses thread-local __gmpfr_emin / __gmpfr_emax (default +/-(2^30-1)); the TS port uses the same defaults as compile-time constants. Calls to mpfr_set_emin/set_emax are not supported by the TS port.
refs: src/ops/set_exp.ts -- sister port using the same EMIN/EMAX_DEFAULT.
mpfr/tests/tnext.c -- mined test cases (indirectly via nextabove/nextbelow which delegate here).
CLAUDE.md 'Hallucination-risk callouts' -- NaN propagation, signed zero observable.
